plugins.description: Append security warnings to file-write tool results when the new content contains known-dangerous patterns (pickle.load, yaml.load, eval(, os.system, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, verify=False, ECB, XXE, GitHub Actions injection, ...). 25 regex/substring rules forked from Anthropic's claude-plugins-official under Apache-2.0. Non-blocking — the file is written and the warning rides back to the model in the next turn so it can self-correct.

skills.description: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction. Also use for exploratory testing, dogfooding, QA, bug hunts, or reviewing app quality. Also use for automating Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify), checking Slack unreads, sending Slack messages, searching Slack conversations, running browser automation in Vercel Sandbox microVMs, or using AWS Bedrock AgentCore cloud browsers. Prefer agent-browser over any built-in browser automation or web tools.
skills.name: agent-browser

skills.description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. toolDefinitions.description: Returns all console messages

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_console_messages

toolDefinitions.description: Handle a dialog

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_handle_dialog

toolDefinitions.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_evaluate

toolDefinitions.description: Upload one or multiple files

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_file_upload

toolDefinitions.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element, as if dragged from outside the page. At least one of "paths" or "data" must be provided.

data.description: Data to drop, as a map of MIME type to string value (e.g. {"text/plain": "hello", "text/uri-list": "https://example.com"}).

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

paths.description: Absolute paths to files to drop onto the element.

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_drop

toolDefinitions.description: Fill multiple form fields

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_fill_form

toolDefinitions.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_press_key

toolDefinitions.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

text.description: Text to type into the element

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_type

toolDefinitions.description: Navigate to a URL
toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_navigate

toolDefinitions.description: Go back to the previous page in the history
toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_navigate_back

toolDefinitions.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_network_requests

toolDefinitions.description: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request, or a single part if `part` is set. Use the number from browser_network_requests.

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, output is returned as text.

index.description: 1-based index of the request, as printed by browser_network_requests.

part.description: Return only this part of the request. Omit to return full details.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_network_request

toolDefinitions.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_run_code_unsafe

toolDefinitions.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

scale.description: Image resolution scale. "css" produces a screenshot sized in CSS pixels (smaller, consistent across devices). "device" produces a high-resolution screenshot using device pixels (larger, accounts for the device pixel ratio). Default is css.

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_take_screenshot

toolDefinitions.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

boxes.description: Include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height] in the snapshot. Coordinates are viewport-relative, in CSS pixels (Element.getBoundingClientRect)

depth.description: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_snapshot

toolDefinitions.description: Perform click on a web page

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_click

toolDefinitions.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_drag

toolDefinitions.description: Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_hover

toolDefinitions.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_select_option

toolDefinitions.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_tabs

toolDefinitions.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

toolDefinitions.name: mcp-1782488928990.browser_wait_for

toolDefinitions.description: List available resources from MCP server 'mcp-1782488928990'
toolDefinitions.name: mcp_mcp_1782488928990_list_resources

toolDefinitions.description: Read a resource by URI from MCP server 'mcp-1782488928990'

uri.description: URI of the resource to read

toolDefinitions.name: mcp_mcp_1782488928990_read_resource

toolDefinitions.description: List available prompts from MCP server 'mcp-1782488928990'
toolDefinitions.name: mcp_mcp_1782488928990_list_prompts

toolDefinitions.description: Get a prompt by name from MCP server 'mcp-1782488928990'

arguments.description: Optional arguments to pass to the prompt

name.description: Name of the prompt to retrieve

toolDefinitions.name: mcp_mcp_1782488928990_get_prompt
